name: Sagittarius A*
description: The supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way galaxy. It anchors our galaxy and has a mass 4.3 million times that of our Sun.

name: M87*
description: Located in the massive elliptical galaxy Messier 87, it was the first black hole ever directly imaged by the Event Horizon Telescope.

name: Cygnus X-1
description: One of the most famous X-ray sources in the sky and the first widely accepted black hole, part of a binary system with a blue supergiant star.

name: TON 618
description: One of the most massive black holes ever discovered, it powers a hyperluminous quasar that shines with the light of 140 trillion suns.

name: V404 Cygni
description: A well-studied microquasar and X-ray binary system that sporadically unleashes massive outbursts as it strips material from its companion star.
